commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `openclaw diagnose --json`, baseline/probe/incident persistence, plugin contract validation, A2A provenance fields, service-wrapper status output, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: current main's `channels.status` contract puts liveness in `channelAccounts`, while the PR counts only `channels` summaries. I did not run the PR branch in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A focused repair can fix the channel-counting defect and add regression coverage; the broader operator API approval remains a maintainer decision.

target.title: Control-plane hardening from Optimus ops findings
target.body:
## Summary

Completes the OpenClaw control-plane hardening project stack from Optimus ops findings:

- ADP-237: structured A2A handoff IDs and return-route tracing.
- ADP-238: session/status diagnostics and safer control-plane visibility.
- ADP-239: channel/runtime boundary checks.
- ADP-240: plugin contract validation surfaces.
- ADP-241: plugin contract validator CLI path.
- ADP-242: non-destructive doctor/probe guardrails.
- ADP-243: unified `openclaw diagnose --json` control-plane API.

Maintainer/ClawSweeper follow-up in latest head `416831cc1ac20856eee4db3d2d1aa8f175b9adb9`:

- Baseline session metrics now use `sessions.list.totalCount` instead of the first returned page length.
- Baseline active-task metrics now page through `tasks.list.nextCursor` for queued/running tasks instead of capping at one page.
- `diagnose --timeout` is threaded into baseline gateway probes.
- `channels.status` map responses are handled without array-only `.filter()` assumptions.
- Legacy root SDK diagnostic-event compatibility test emits and observes a real diagnostic event.
- Diagnose proof was refreshed from the current head.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Validate the new control-plane hardening surfaces and the ClawSweeper repairs on current head `416831cc1ac20856eee4db3d2d1aa8f175b9adb9`.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw development checkout on this PR branch. Commands were run from the repository root after rebuilding the branch. No destructive doctor repairs, service restarts, live plugin updates, production channel startup, or credential rotation were run.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts`
2. `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core`
3. `CI=true pnpm lint:core`
4. `CI=true pnpm lint --threads=8`
5. `pnpm build`
6. `node dist/entry.js plugins contracts validate --strict --json`
7. `timeout 30s node dist/entry.js diagnose --json --timeout 1000`
8. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts`
9. `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 14.75s
```

```text
$ CI=true pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```

```text
$ CI=true pnpm lint:core
[plugin-sdk boundary root shims] fresh; skipping
```

```text
$ CI=true pnpm lint --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished
```

```text
$ pnpm build
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.
```

```json
$ node dist/entry.js plugins contracts validate --strict --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "strict": true,
  "pluginCount": 125,
  "findingCount": 30
}
```

```json
$ timeout 30s node dist/entry.js diagnose --json --timeout 1000
{
  "ok": true,
  "baselineMetrics": {
    "sessionCount": 30,
    "agentCount": 0,
    "channelCount": 1,
    "activeTaskCount": 0
  },
  "baselineComponents": {
    "gateway": "pass",
    "channels": "fail",
    "agents": "pass",
    "tasks": "pass",
    "locks": "pass",
    "plugins": "pass"
  },
  "gatewayMessage": "Gateway responded",
  "channelMessage": "0/1 channels connected",
  "taskMessage": "0 active tasks",
  "pluginsMessage": "125 plugins loaded",
  "probeCache": {
    "count": 1,
    "stale": 0,
    "recent": [
      {
        "type": "plugin",
        "id": "contracts",
        "stale": false
      }
    ]
  },
  "incidents": {
    "count": 0,
    "open": 0,
    "frozen": 0,
    "repairs": 0
  }
}
```

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
```

```text
$ git diff --check
(no output)
```

Observed result after fix: `diagnose --json` now reports current baseline session count from the gateway total count and active task count from a paginated task scan. The previous `channels.status` `.filter()` failure no longer appears; the channel component now reports the actual current local state (`0/1 channels connected`). Plugin contract validation and diagnose both complete with `ok: true`, and diagnose records `diagnose-latest`, probe-cache, baseline, task, and incident summary evidence without running destructive repair actions.

What was not tested: I did not run destructive doctor repairs, service restarts, live plugin updates, production channel startup, or credential rotation. Those are intentionally unsafe actions and outside this PR's non-destructive validation path.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core`
- `CI=true pnpm lint:core`
- `CI=true pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm build`
- `node dist/entry.js plugins contracts validate --strict --json`
- `timeout 30s node dist/entry.js diagnose --json --timeout 1000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

The upstream repository is creator/maintainer-owned, so I did not attempt to merge or bypass review. This update is limited to the PR branch, proof refresh, and re-review request.


Observed result after fix: `diagnose --json` now reports current baseline session count from the gateway total count and active task count from a paginated task scan. The previous `channels.status` `.filter()` failure no longer appears; the channel component now reports the actual current local state (`0/1 channels connected`). Plugin contract validation and diagnose both complete with `ok: true`, and diagnose records `diagnose-latest`, probe-cache, baseline, task, and incident summary evidence without running destructive repair actions.

What was not tested: I did not run destructive doctor repairs, service restarts, live plugin updates, production channel startup, or credential rotation. Those are intentionally unsafe actions and outside this PR's non-destructive validation path.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core`
- `CI=true pnpm lint:core`
- `CI=true pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm build`
- `node dist/entry.js plugins contracts validate --strict --json`
- `timeout 30s node dist/entry.js diagnose --json --timeout 1000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

The upstream repository is creator/maintainer-owned, so I did not attempt to merge or bypass review. This update is limited to the PR branch, proof refresh, and re-review request.
